code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # simple verb mapping for readability
    verb_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of",
        "location": "is located at",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayName": "has runway named",
        "country": "is in",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has elevation of",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code",
        "nativeName": "has native name",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to party",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has elevation of",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement",
        "mayor": "has mayor",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has second runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has third runway surface of",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface of",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language",
        "city": "is city of",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter at",
        "class": "belongs to class",
        "division": "belongs to division",
        "order": "belongs to order",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft",
        "battle": "participated in battle",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fifth runway surface of",
        "countySeat": "has county seat",
        "chief": "has chief",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "areaCode": "has area code",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county",
    }
    # Build clauses per subject, combining predicates for the same subject
    # optional units for numeric values
    unit_map = {
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "metres",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "feet",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "metres",
        "runwayLength": "metres",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "metres",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "feet",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "feet",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "",  # no unit
    }

    # First collect raw triples per subject
    raw_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        raw_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, tlist in raw_map.items():
        # Special combination: cityServed + country -> “serves the city of X which is in Y”
        city = None
        country = None
        other_phrases: list[str] = []
        for t in tlist:
            pred = t.predicate
            obj = t.object.strip()
            # strip surrounding quotes
            if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
                obj = obj[1:-1]

            # numeric handling
            try:
                num = float(obj)
                unit = unit_map.get(pred, "")
                if unit:
                    if num.is_integer():
                        obj = f"{int(num)} {unit}"
                    else:
                        obj = f"{num:g} {unit}"
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass

            if pred == "cityServed":
                city = obj
                continue
            if pred == "country":
                country = obj
                continue

            verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
            other_phrases.append(f"{verb} {obj}")

        # Build main clause for this subject
        if city and country:
            main_phrase = f"serves the city of {city} which is in {country}"
        elif city:
            main_phrase = f"serves the city of {city}"
        elif country:
            main_phrase = f"is in {country}"
        else:
            main_phrase = ""

        # Combine main phrase with other predicates
        all_phrases = [main_phrase] if main_phrase else []
        all_phrases.extend(other_phrases)

        if not all_phrases:
            continue

        if len(all_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {all_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            if len(all_phrases) == 2:
                joined = " and ".join(all_phrases)
            else:
                joined = ", ".join(all_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + all_phrases[-1]
            clause = f"{subj} {joined}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    if not clauses:
        return ""
    # Build a single complex sentence from all clauses
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        if len(clauses) == 2:
            sentence = " and ".join(clauses)
        else:
            sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

        if not all_phrases:
            continue

        if len(all_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {all_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            if len(all_phrases) == 2:
                joined = " and ".join(all_phrases)
            else:
                joined = ", ".join(all_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + all_phrases[-1]
            clause = f"{subj} {joined}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    if not clauses:
        return ""
    # Build a single complex sentence from all clauses
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        if len(clauses) == 2:
            sentence = " and ".join(clauses)
        else:
            sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        return sentence + "."
